zh.lifeSummary: 當William De Winter I於1220年出生在英國威爾斯格拉莫爾根時，他的父親OWEN WYNTOUR30歲，母親Elizabeth verch Meurig of Ystrad14歲。他於1245年與Margaret Jordan在英國威爾斯結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1290年在英國威爾斯布洛可爾享年70歲。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Winter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English (Old English) personal name Winter (Old English Wintra), originally a nickname from the word for ‘winter’ and perhaps still a nickname (see 2 below) in the period of surname formation.
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name from Middle English winter, a variant of Middle English, Anglo-Norman French viniter, vinter ‘wine merchant’.
Irish: Anglicized form (part translation) of Gaelic Mac Giolla-Gheimhridh ‘son of the lad of winter’, from geimhreadh ‘winter’. This name is also Anglicized McAlivery.

variants.name: Winters
meanings: Dutch and German: patronymic from   Winter  .
English: variant of Winter , with post-medieval excrescent -s.
Irish: adopted for Mac Giolla Gheimhridh ‘son of the lad of winter’, from geimhreadh ‘winter’. Compare Winter 4.

variants.name: Winther
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Niels, Aksel, Anders, Bent, Jorgen, Lars, Nels, Sven.  German Gerhardt, Kurt, Otto.
German, Danish, and Swedish: variant of   Winter  .

variants.name: Winterberg
meanings: German: habitational name from any of several places called with Middle High German winter ‘winter’ + berg ‘mountain, hill’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name composed of German Winter + Berg ‘mountain, hill’.

variants.name: Winterman
meanings: English (Kent and Middlesex): occupational name or status name denoting a servant of a man named   Winter   1.
English (Kent and Middlesex): alternatively, perhaps a nickname denoting a person with a cold, wintry appearance or disposition.

English (Kent and Middlesex): occasionally perhaps an occupational name for a wine merchant or a wine merchant's servant, from a variant of Middle English and Anglo-Norman French viniter, vinter ‘wine merchant’ (modern English vintner) + man ‘man’.

variants.name: Wynter
meanings: Scottish (West Lothian): variant of   Winter  .

variants.name: De Winter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Urbain, Marcel.
Dutch; Flemish (also Dewinter): variant of Winter + definite article de, particularly as a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or tavern named with the season.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William De Winter I was born in 1220, in Glamorgan, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, OWEN WYNTOUR, was 30 and his mother, Elizabeth verch Meurig of Ystrad, was 14. He married Margaret Jordan in 1245, in Wales, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1290, in Pembrokeshire, Wales, United Kingdom, at the age of 70.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William De Winter I est né en 1220, à Glamorgan, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, OWEN WYNTOUR, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth verch Meurig of Ystrad, avait 14 ans. Il a épousé Margaret Jordan en 1245, à Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1290, à Pembrokeshire, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: William De Winter I(이)가 1220년 영국 웨일스 글램르건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 OWEN WYNTOUR(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth verch Meurig of Ystrad(은)는 14세였다. 그는 1245년 영국 웨일스에서 Margaret Jordan(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1290년 영국 웨일스 p브로케실르에서 70세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Jordan в 1245 г. в местности Уэльс, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
